title: Add --sync option to podman rm
body:
With the changes made recently to ensure Podman does not hit the OCI runtime as often to sync state, we can find ourselves in a situation where the runtime's state does not match ours.

Add a --sync flag to podman rm to ensure we can still remove containers when this happens.

comments.body: /approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@baude @rhatdan @umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
LGTM
Not sure how I managed to "request changes" on that review comment....
Added --sync to `ps` as well, so we can fix the entire state at once
What problem does this solve? I'm trying to wrap my head around why I need this as a user
@baude If a container is deleted from runc, we can get Podman into a state where containers can't be removed or stopped or killed without a restart. This fixes that.

In stead of adding an option, could we look for this error and trigger a sync and attempt a second time.
Why bother the user with a hard to find option?

We'd need to parse error messages out of the OCI runtime. We can maybe do that with runc, but for other runtimes (Kata) we can't guarantee the errors will be the same, so we'll still need the explicit option to trigger it.
How about on any failure you sync and try again?  Second failure you report.
Be a lot of code if we want to do that for all our big commands (start, kill, stop, rm at a minimum I think), but that could work.
I just don't see this command being used, unless we the user has a failure and we say try this random command, which seems like a failure in the tool.

I think you're probably right. The only issue I can see is `ps` showing inaccurate information which we can't flush without trying another command (there are no errors in `ps` to retry after because we never call runc)
@rhatdan What do you think about leaving this in for `ps` and adding sync-retry for `start`, `stop`, `rm`, `kill`
That is fine with me. Although I expect 0 people to ever use it.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1714) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@rhatdan @baude I don't think the full version of this with retries will be ready for a release today.

Proposal: I strip the flag out of `rm` and leave the code for calling it in `ps`, so we can tell people what to do if they manage to explode their state, and we fix things the right way for next release.
@rhatdan @baude @vrothberg @giuseppe @umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL - would like this merged prior to release
F28 Cloud isn't happy, but I think that's (unfortunately) expected today due to unrelated CI issues.
Podman run kernel-memory test... I remember us having a lot of trouble with this one a long while ago, when we were initially porting over from the CRI-O repo
bot, retest this please
LGTM, assuming happy tests
bot, retest this please
LGTM
LGTM with a minor nitpick that the rm commits could be removed.
F28 cloud does appear to be broken. Very consistently failing on the kernel memory test.
`process_linux.go:311: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"failed to set memory.kmem.limit_in_bytes, because either tasks have already joined this cgroup or it has children\"`
@giuseppe Did we update runc commit recently? I think I remember that happening.

Pushed a commit to update runc again, it looks like the most recent PR merged fixed our issue.
Anyone want to /lgtm?
Aw, hell - latest runc doesn't seem to have fixed it
@baude Rebased. Mind dropping an LGTM?
Flake looks like it was just our old friend, mountinfo being invalid.
/retest
